paper_title: Optimization of condition for pectin extraction from pomelo peel using response surface methodology

The process of isolating pectin from plant materials, specifically pomelo peel.
Determining the best conditions for the extraction process to maximize yield.
An extraction technique using ultrasonic waves to improve yield and reduce time.
A mathematical and statistical technique for empirical model building.
An experimental design for response surface methodology.
The chemical solvent used for the pectin extraction process.
Statistical software used for optimization and data analysis.
The matrix of 27 experiments and their resulting data points.
The highest predicted pectin yield achieved under optimal conditions.
The actual yield obtained from validation experiments.
The coefficient of determination indicating the goodness of fit of the model.
Statistical significance value for the developed model.
The specific pH level of citric acid for maximum yield.
The specific duration of ultrasound treatment for maximum yield.
The specific extraction temperature for maximum yield.
